revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ジャングル風呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ジャングル風呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ジャングル風呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ジャングル風呂]]
[[File:Kanseikaku.jpg|thumb|240x240px|An example of a jungle bath<small>{{ill|Harazuru Onsen Kanseikaku|de|Harazuru Onsen Kanseikaku|ja|原鶴温泉咸生閣|zh|原鹤温泉咸生阁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harazuru Onsen Kanseikaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579539|1=Harazuru Onsen Kanseikaku}} (2004-08-28)</small>]] '''Janguruburo''' (ジャングル風呂) is a large-scale bathing facility in which various types of tubs—such as {{ill|large communal bath|de|große Gemeinschaftsbäder|ja|大浴場|1=Daiyokujō|2=de|3=große Gemeinschaftsbäder|4=ja|5=大浴場|1=Daiyokujō|6=zh|7=大型公共浴池|1=Daiyokujō|zh|大型公共浴池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/large communal bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiyokujō|qq=}} and {{ill|family bath|de|Familienbad|ja|家族風呂|1=Family Bath|zh|家庭浴池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/family bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Family Bath|qq=}}—are installed inside a greenhouse lush with tropical plants. Users are, in principle, required to be naked, just as in a standard {{ill|Public bathing|en|Public bathing|qid=Q785952|lt=Public Bathhouse|1=Public bathing}}. In some facilities, {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=hot spring water|1=Onsen}} is {{ill|Drawn‐off hot spring|ja|引湯|lt=Drawn‐off|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Drawn‐off hot spring|qq=}} from nearby springs.
== Overview == A type of large-scale bathing facility often installed in {{ill|health land|de|Gesundheitsland|ja|健康ランド|zh|健康乐园|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/health land|qq=}} or large-scale {{ill|onsen ryokan|de|Onsen-Ryokan|ja|温泉旅館|zh|温泉旅馆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/onsen ryokan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉宿|1=onsen ryokan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11562938}}. These are typically built in daylight-lit buildings of at least 150 tsubo (roughly 495 m²). Within the bathing area, artificial hills and tropical gardens are installed, or a shallow, circulating {{ill|Bathtub|en|Bathtub|qid=Q152095|lt=bath pool|1=Bathtub}} runs around the entire space to simulate a tropical {{ill|Tropical rainforest|en|Tropical rainforest|qid=Q199403|lt=jungle rainforest|1=Tropical rainforest}}. Facilities often include artificial {{ill|Waterfall|en|Waterfall|qid=Q34038|lt=waterfalls|1=Waterfall}} or exploration-style {{ill|Cave|en|Cave|qid=Q35509|lt=caves|1=Cave}} to add entertainment value for children, though many advertise {{ill|Mixed bathing|en|Mixed bathing|qid=Q6883947|lt=mixed bathing|1=Mixed bathing}} as a draw<ref name="the sun">『太陽』1976年1月号 NO.152「九州へんな温泉旅」藤原審爾</ref>.
A common feature is a water spout modeled after the face of a {{ill|Lion|en|Lion|qid=Q140|lt=lion|1=Lion}} or a {{ill|Tiger|en|Tiger|qid=Q19939|lt=tiger|1=Tiger}}. Until relatively recently, many communal taps were imported from Britain; in Europe since the Middle Ages, the lion has been regarded as the guardian deity of water (in contrast to Japan and China, where the dragon serves that role, giving rise to the term "蛇口" ["faucet"]).
In terms of enjoying scenery and atmosphere, these share elements with {{ill|outdoor baths|de|Freiluftbad|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|zh|露天浴池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/outdoor baths|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}} and indoor {{ill|rock baths|de|Felsenbad|ja|岩風呂|zh|岩浴池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rock baths|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q135583587|1=Q135583587}}. However, because they require labor-intensive care for living plants, they have gradually disappeared. Some onsen hotels, however, actively promote and utilize them as entertainment facilities.
Their heyday was during the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}}, making them objects of retro or Shōwa nostalgia. From around 1965 to 1975, many health centers featured jungle baths.
The author {{ill|Shinji Fujiwara|de|Shinji Fujiwara|ja|藤原審爾|zh|藤原審爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Fujiwara|1=Shinji Fujiwara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623205}}, who had extensive {{ill|hot spring therapy|de|Heilbad|ja|湯治|zh|温泉疗养|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring therapy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Thermal bath|qid=Q1004887}} experience in his youth due to {{ill|pulmonary tuberculosis|de|Lungentuberkulose|ja|肺結核|zh|肺结核|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pulmonary tuberculosis|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結核|1=Tuberculosis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q12204}}, contributed an account of his stay at the jungle bath of the Ibusuki Kankō Hotel to the January 1976 issue of the magazine 『太陽』. Although he had thought {{ill|Ibusuki|en|Ibusuki|qid=Q19712|lt=Ibusuki|1=Ibusuki}} to be as remote and obscure as {{ill|Sukumo, Kōchi|en|Sukumo, Kōchi|qid=Q655258|lt=Sukumo|1=Sukumo, Kōchi}} on Shikoku, the large tourist hotel was noisy as if he had stayed at a station, offering nothing like tranquility. He noted that the jungle bath had about thirty separate baths, and its main attraction was the mixed bathing: a lively group of young women made it worth a look, although the women's-only bath was occupied by men, so that roughly 80% of users were men and children, 10% were older women, and the remaining 10% were mothers with children<ref name="the sun"/>.
== Origin == The jungle bath is said to have originated at {{ill|Ibusuki Onsen|en|Ibusuki Onsen|qid=Q5984938|lt=Ibusuki Hot Springs|1=Ibusuki Onsen}}. The first jungle bath, installed at the Ibusuki Kankō Hotel, was recognized as a local specialty of Ibusuki<ref>{{Cite book ja-jp| author = 武田幸一 |year = 1973| title = カラーブックス23『九州の旅』| publisher = 保育社| pages = 90}}</ref>. Another theory credits the villa of ornithologist {{ill|Masauji Hachisuka|en|Masauji Hachisuka|qid=Q11625684|lt=Masashi Hachisuka|1=Masauji Hachisuka}} in {{ill|Atami|en|Atami|qid=Q653364|lt=Atami|1=Atami}} (built 1933, demolished 1986), where a large bath was planted with tropical flora, as the true origin<ref>{{Cite book ja-jp| author = [[藤森照信]],[[荒俣宏]]| title =『東京路上博物誌』| publisher = [[鹿島出版会]] | year=1987| isbn = 4-306-09303-4}}</ref>.
== Existing Facilities == === In Japan === * {{ill|Hyakuketsu Onsen Haruna|de|Hyakuketsu Onsen Haruna|ja|百穴温泉 春奈|zh|百穴温泉 春奈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyakuketsu Onsen Haruna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579540|1=Hyakuketsu Onsen Haruna}} (Hiki District, Yoshimi, Saitama) * {{ill|Atagawa Onsen Hotel Katara Fukushima-ya|de|Atagawa Onsen Hotel Katara Fukushima-ya|ja|熱川温泉 ホテル カターラ福島屋|zh|热川温泉 卡塔拉福岛屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagawa Onsen Hotel Katara Fukushima-ya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579541|1=Atagawa Onsen Hotel Katara Fukushima-ya}} (Kamo District, Higashiizu, Shizuoka) * {{ill|Atagawa Onsen Hotel Shinayoshi|de|Atagawa Onsen Hotel Shinayoshi|ja|熱川温泉 ホテル志なよし|zh|热川温泉 志菜良|zh|热川温泉 志菜良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagawa Onsen Hotel Shinayoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579542|1=Atagawa Onsen Hotel Shinayoshi}} (Kamo District, Higashiizu, Shizuoka) * {{ill|Minshuku Hagoromo-so|de|Minshuku Hagoromo-so|ja|民宿はごろも荘|zh|民宿羽衣庄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minshuku Hagoromo-so|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579543|1=Minshuku Hagoromo-so}} (Numazu, Shizuoka) * {{ill|Yukai Resort Koshinoyu|de|Yukai Resort Koshinoyu|ja|湯快リゾート 越之湯|zh|汤快度假村 越之汤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukai Resort Koshinoyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579544|1=Yukai Resort Koshinoyu}} (Nachi-Katsuura, Wakayama) – closed due to fire * {{ill|Shiawase no Mura|de|Shiawase no Mura|ja|しあわせの村|zh|幸福村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiawase no Mura|1=Village of Happiness KOBE|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11268717}} (Kita-ku, Kobe, Hyōgo) * {{ill|Kanseikaku Jungle Bath Harazuru Onsen|de|Kanseikaku Jungle Bath Harazuru Onsen|ja|原鶴温泉 泰泉閣 ジャングル風呂|zh|原鹤温泉 泰泉阁 丛林浴场|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanseikaku Jungle Bath Harazuru Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579545|1=Kanseikaku Jungle Bath Harazuru Onsen}} (Asakura, Fukuoka) * {{ill|Kirishima Castle Hotel|de|Kirishima Castle Hotel|ja|霧島温泉 ホテル霧島キャッスル|zh|雾岛温泉 雾岛城堡酒店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Castle Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579546|1=Kirishima Castle Hotel}} (Kirishima, Kagoshima) * {{ill|Hotel Maruzen|de|Hotel Maruzen|ja|ホテル マル善|zh|丸善酒店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotel Maruzen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579547|1=Hotel Maruzen}} (Satsumasendai, Kagoshima) * {{ill|Kakino Onsen Hasshoen Yumoto-kan|de|Kakino Onsen Hasshoen Yumoto-kan|ja|柿野温泉 八勝園湯本館|zh|柿野温泉 八胜园汤本馆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakino Onsen Hasshoen Yumoto-kan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579548|1=Kakino Onsen Hasshoen Yumoto-kan}} (Toki, Gifu)
=== Outside Japan === * {{ill|Puyeiye Hot Springs Hotel|de|Puyeiye Hot Springs Hotel|ja|プイエイエ温泉ホテル|zh|普耶耶温泉酒店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Puyeiye Hot Springs Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579549|1=Hotel Termas Puyehue Wellness & Spa Resort}} (Chile) * {{ill|Zhiben Hot Springs Easy Garden Hotel|de|Zhiben Hot Springs Easy Garden Hotel|ja|知本温泉 逸園大霧島飯店|zh|知本温泉 逸园大雾岛饭店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhiben Hot Springs Easy Garden Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579551|1=Zhiben Hot Springs Easy Garden Hotel}} (Taiwan)
== Former Facilities == * {{ill|Toya Sun Palace|de|Toya Sun Palace|ja|洞爺温泉 サンパレス洞爺|zh|洞爷温泉 太阳宫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toya Sun Palace|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579552|1=Toya Sun Palace}} (Tōyako, Hokkaido) * {{ill|Kawayu Leisure Land|de|Kawayu Leisure Land|ja|屈斜路温泉 いなせレジャーランド|zh|屈斜路温泉 豪爽休闲乐园|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Leisure Land|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579553|1=Kawayu Leisure Land}} (Kussharo, Hokkaido) * {{ill|Akita Hawaiian Center|de|Akita Hawaiian Center|ja|秋田ハワイアンセンター|zh|秋田夏威夷中心|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Hawaiian Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579554|1=Akita Hawaiian Center}} (Akita) * {{ill|Hotel Hawaii|de|Hotel Hawaii|ja|秋田温泉 ホテル ハワイ|zh|秋田温泉 夏威夷酒店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotel Hawaii|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579555|1=Hotel Hawaii}} (Akita, Chūō Ward) * {{ill|Akiu Leisure Center|de|Akiu Leisure Center|ja|秋保レジャーセンター|zh|秋保休闲中心|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiu Leisure Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579556|1=Akiu Leisure Center}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai, Miyagi) * {{ill|Matsushima Onsen Health Center|de|Matsushima Onsen Health Center|ja|松島温泉ヘルスセンター|zh|松岛温泉健康中心|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushima Onsen Health Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579557|1=Matsushima Onsen Health Center}} (Matsushima) * {{ill|Yamagata Hawaiian Dream Land|de|Yamagata Hawaiian Dream Land|ja|飯田温泉 山形ハワイドリームランド|zh|饭田温泉 山形夏威夷梦幻乐园|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Hawaiian Dream Land|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579558|1=Yamagata Hawaiian Dream Land}} (Yamagata) – designed by {{ill|Kisho Kurokawa|en|Kisho Kurokawa|qid=Q358502|lt=Kisho Kurokawa|1=Kisho Kurokawa}}. * {{ill|Tokiwa Hawaiian Center|de|Tokiwa Hawaiian Center|ja|常磐ハワイアンセンター|zh|常磐夏威夷中心|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokiwa Hawaiian Center|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スパリゾートハワイアンズ|1=Spa Resort Hawaiians|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7572157}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) * {{ill|Hotel Jungle Palace|de|Hotel Jungle Palace|ja|ホテル ジャングルパレス|zh|丛林宫殿酒店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotel Jungle Palace|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579559|1=Hotel Jungle Palace}} (Minamibōsō, Chiba) * {{ill|Hakone Kowakien|de|Hakone Kowakien|ja|箱根小涌園|zh|箱根小涌园|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakone Kowakien|1=Hakone Kowakien|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11603366}} (Hakone) * {{ill|Eihōkaku|de|Eihōkaku|ja|永芳閣|zh|永芳阁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eihōkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579560|1=Eihōkaku}} (Himi, Toyama) * {{ill|Tōjinbō Family Land|de|Tōjinbō Family Land|ja|東尋坊温泉ファミリーランド|zh|东寻坊温泉家庭乐园|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōjinbō Family Land|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三国オーシャンリゾート&ホテル|1=Mikuni Ocean Resort & Hotel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11354771}} (Mikuni, Fukui) * {{ill|Matsumoto Health Center|de|Matsumoto Health Center|ja|松本ヘルスセンター|zh|松本健康中心|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Health Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579561|1=Matsumoto Health Center}} (Matsumoto, Nagano) * {{ill|Togura Kamiyamada Onsen Seifuen|de|Togura Kamiyamada Onsen Seifuen|ja|戸倉上山田温泉清風園|zh|户仓上山田温泉清风园|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togura Kamiyamada Onsen Seifuen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579562|1=Togura Kamiyamada Onsen Seifuen}} (Chikuma, Nagano) * {{ill|Atagawa Daiichi Hotel|de|Atagawa Daiichi Hotel|ja|熱川温泉 熱川第一ホテル|zh|热川温泉 第一酒店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagawa Daiichi Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579563|1=Atagawa Daiichi Hotel}} (Higashiizu, Shizuoka) * {{ill|Teisan Health Center|de|Teisan Health Center|ja|帝産ヘルスセンター|zh|帝产健康中心|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teisan Health Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579564|1=Teisan Health Center}} (Izu, Shizuoka) * {{ill|Kowa Leisure Land|de|Kowa Leisure Land|ja|コーワレジャーランド|zh|兴和休闲乐园|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowa Leisure Land|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579566|1=Kowa Leisure Land}} (Tobishima, Aichi) * {{ill|Miya Onsen Matsukazuen|de|Miya Onsen Matsukazuen|ja|三谷温泉 松風園|zh|三谷温泉 松风园|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miya Onsen Matsukazuen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579567|1=Miya Onsen Matsukazuen}} (Gamagōri, Aichi) * {{ill|Biwako Paradise|de|Biwako Paradise|ja|びわ湖パラダイス|zh|琵琶湖天堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biwako Paradise|1=Biwako Paradise|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11276862}} (Ōtsu, Shiga) * {{ill|PL Land|de|PL Land|ja|PLランド|zh|PL乐园|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/PL Land|1=PL LAND|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11537701}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Ikenobo Mangetsujō|de|Ikenobo Mangetsujō|ja|池之坊満月城|zh|池之坊满月城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikenobo Mangetsujō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579568|1=Ikenobo Mangetsujō}} (Arima Onsen, Hyōgo)<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://www.bousaihaku.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2017/03/a050.pdf "特異火災事例 池之坊満月城"]}} 消防防災博物館（一般財団法人 消防防災科学センター）。2020年5月25日閲覧。</ref> * {{ill|Hawai Onsen Hagoromo|de|Hawai Onsen Hagoromo|ja|はわい温泉 羽衣|zh|羽衣温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hawai Onsen Hagoromo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579569|1=Hawai Onsen Hagoromo}} (Yurihama, Tottori) * {{ill|Oku-Dōgo Jungle Onsen|de|Oku-Dōgo Jungle Onsen|ja|ホテル奥道後 ジャングル温泉|zh|奥道后丛林温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oku-Dōgo Jungle Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579570|1=Oku-Dōgo Jungle Onsen}} – converted to an outdoor bath in 2014 due to aging. * {{ill|Kanseikaku|de|Kanseikaku|ja|原鶴温泉 咸生閣|zh|原鹤温泉 咸生阁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanseikaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579572|1=Kanseikaku}} – closed March 13, 2020; bankrupt March 31, 2020.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.tsr-net.co.jp/news/tsr/20200331_04.html |title=（株）原鶴温泉咸生閣 |website=TSR速報（大型倒産情報・注目企業動向） |publisher=[[東京商工リサーチ]] |date=2020-03-31 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200401095626/https://www.tsr-net.co.jp/news/tsr/20200331_04.html |archivedate=2020-04-01 |accessdate=2020-04-01}}</ref> * {{ill|Nagasaki Health Center|de|Nagasaki Health Center|ja|長崎ヘルスセンター|zh|长崎健康中心|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Health Center|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579573|1=Nagasaki Health Center}} * {{ill|Beppu Suginoi Palace|de|Beppu Suginoi Palace|ja|別府杉乃井ホテル スギノイパレス 花の大温泉|zh|别府杉乃井酒店 杉乃井宫 花之大温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu Suginoi Palace|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579575|1=Beppu Suginoi Palace}} (Beppu, Ōita) * {{ill|Toi Misaki Sango-sō|de|Toi Misaki Sango-sō|ja|都井岬 民宿 サンゴ荘 ジャングル風呂|zh|都井岬民宿珊瑚庄 丛林浴场|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toi Misaki Sango-sō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579576|1=Toi Misaki Sango-sō}} (Kushima, Miyazaki) * {{ill|Ibusuki Kankō Hotel|de|Ibusuki Kankō Hotel|ja|指宿観光ホテル|zh|指宿观光酒店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibusuki Kankō Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579577|1=Ibusuki Kankō Hotel}} (Ibusuki, Kagoshima) * {{ill|Ibusuki Seaside Hotel|de|Ibusuki Seaside Hotel|ja|指宿シーサイドホテル|zh|指宿滨海酒店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibusuki Seaside Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579578|1=Ibusuki Seaside Hotel}} * {{ill|Kirishima Kankō Hotel|de|Kirishima Kankō Hotel|ja|霧島観光ホテル|zh|雾岛观光酒店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Kankō Hotel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579579|1=Kirishima Kankō Hotel}}
== References == {{footnotes}} <references />
